reason: Close the cheap hypothesis that the grouped-D direct-source TileLang kernel was slow only because it recomputed P/dP for too many D groups.

sweep.read: Least shared-state pressure but most P/dP recompute; slowest point.
Best point, but only a 2.7% improvement over DPK2 and still far from useful.
Full-D reuse regresses, likely from shared-state/register/LDS pressure and the unresolved group_acc race warning.

comparison_gates.read: Even the best S512 grouped-D direct-source point is slower than S4096-level gates from other branches, so it should not be run as an end-to-end TFLOPs candidate.

decision.summary: Increasing D-block reuse does not rescue the grouped-D direct-source TileLang schedule. The bottleneck is not just D-group recomputation; this lowering is schedule-bound and still carries TileLang group_acc data-race warnings.
decision.next_branch: Move back to the proven rocWMMA/HIP contribution-tile surface or CK-Tile/rocPRIM-style segmented reducer: preserve query-major MFMA contribution production, reduce only active touched groups, and avoid dense local tables or HSK materialization.
